@@ -1451,7 +1451,7 @@
 {"id":"wa-wb6","title":"[Human command] `wa backup import` (restore with verification and safety checks)","description":"# Task: wa backup import

## Goal
Safely restore data from backup archives with verification and user confirmation.

## Command Design
```bash
# Verify backup without importing
$ wa backup import backup.wa --verify
Backup verified:
  Version: 0.1.0 (compatible)
  Created: 2026-01-18T14:30:15Z
  Segments: 12,345
  Events: 567
  Checksum: OK

# Dry-run to see what would change
$ wa backup import backup.wa --dry-run
Would restore:
  + 12,345 segments (current: 8,000)
  + 567 events (current: 300)
  Config: 3 differences

# Full restore (replaces current)
$ wa backup import backup.wa --replace
⚠️  This will replace ALL current data!
Current data will be backed up to: ~/.local/share/wa/backups/pre_import_2026-01-18.wa
Continue? [y/N]

# Merge mode (keep newer, import older)
$ wa backup import backup.wa --merge
Merging...
  Imported: 4,345 segments
  Skipped: 8,000 (newer local)
  Conflicts: 0
```

## Safety Requirements
1. **Automatic pre-import backup**: Before any destructive import, create backup of current state
2. **Checksum verification**: Reject imports with invalid checksums
3. **Schema compatibility**: Check version and migrate if needed
4. **Explicit confirmation**: Require --yes or interactive confirmation for destructive ops
5. **Watcher coordination**: Require watcher to be stopped, or use --force with warning

## Error Handling
```bash
$ wa backup import corrupted.wa
Error: Backup verification failed
  Expected checksum: abc123
  Actual checksum: def456
  
Backup may be corrupted. If you trust the source:
  wa backup import corrupted.wa --skip-verify --force
```

## Testing
- Unit tests: verification logic, merge algorithm
- Integration: import into empty DB, import with conflicts
- E2E: full restore cycle, error on corrupt backup

## Acceptance Criteria
- Import verifies checksum before any changes
- Pre-import backup created automatically
- Dry-run mode shows exactly what would change
- Merge mode preserves newer local data
- Clear errors for corrupt/incompatible backups

 {"id":"wa-wgeh","title":"E2E: prioritized capture under load","description":"## Scenarios
- Simulate two panes (high vs low priority) under heavy output
- Verify high-priority pane has more frequent captures
- Verify throttling events are emitted

## Logging
- Capture per-pane capture intervals
- Include throttle reasons in artifacts

## Success Criteria

 {"id":"wa-wo94","title":"Profile format + storage layout","description":"## What
Define how profiles are stored on disk and referenced.

## Why
A predictable layout is required for tooling and safe updates.

## How
- Decide path: `~/.config/wa/profiles/<name>.toml`
- Define profile metadata (description, last_updated)
- Ensure default profile remains implicit

## Success Criteria
- Profiles can be discovered without scanning the whole config dir
- Format is forward-compatible","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:06:24.584288556Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.190878-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-01T18:05:20.149861483Z","close_reason":"Drafted config profile format/storage spec","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-wo94","depends_on_id":"wa-ts1a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"wa-wqpd","title":"Renderers + TUI/web: show annotations","description":"## What
Update renderers and UI surfaces to display annotations and triage state.

## Why
Annotations are useless if not visible or filterable in common views.

## How
- CLI renderers include note/label/state columns in verbose mode
- TUI: detail view shows notes/labels and triage actions
- Web: read-only annotation display and filters

## Success Criteria
- Rendering stays concise in non-verbose output
- Filters by label/state work in UI","notes":"Implemented annotation/triage rendering surfaces across CLI/TUI/web. CLI: wa events --verbose now appends an annotation table (event/triage/labels/note) while keeping default concise output unchanged. TUI: event query now loads annotations; Events detail panel displays triage state, labels, and note. Web: /events now includes optional annotations object (triage_state, note, labels) and retains triage/label filters. Validation passed: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, plus wa-core feature checks with --features "tui web".","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:03:31.861081659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:47:25.862365-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:47:25.862374-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-wqpd","depends_on_id":"wa-1yk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-wqpd","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-wqpd","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"}]}
